open_questions: Safehold related-party management alignment and post-2027-03-31 manager contest timing.
Promote McIntyre Q1 2026 letter if acceptable as approved context.
Confirm SAFE high-case rate re-rating path stays sensitivity-only.
thesis: Star Holdings (STHO) is a Nasdaq-listed wind-down vehicle created in March 2023 when iStar Inc. merged with Safehold Inc. and spun off legacy non-ground-lease assets. **[Fact]** The company holds loans, operating properties, land (Asbury Park Waterfront and Magnolia Green), and approximately 13.5 million shares of Safehold Inc. (NYSE: SAFE). **[Fact]** It is externally managed by a wholly owned Safehold subsidiary and aims to maximize cash through asset sales, loan repayments, and share repurchases rather than dividends. **[Fact]** At March 31, 2026, total assets were $480.4 million, stockholders' equity was $240.2 million (~$19.88 per share on 12.08 million shares), cash and restricted cash were $62.1 million, and long-term debt was $207.0 million. **[Fact]** The Safe stake was marked at $183.0 million fair value ($13.53 per SAFE share).
why_market_wrong: The market prices STHO like a broken small-cap REIT with volatile GAAP losses, not like a dated capital-return vehicle sitting on a proof-backed component value near **$18 per share** versus a quote near **$9**. **[Inference]** Q1 2026 reported a $10.3 million net loss driven largely by non-cash Safehold mark-to-market moves, which obscures loan repayments and balance-sheet simplification. **[Inference]** Complexity (VIE history, margin loan on the Safe stake, related-party Safehold management) keeps generalist investors away. The March 27, 2026 deconsolidation of the Asbury seller-financed venture (mezzanine repaid, guaranty released) reduced reported assets but improved net cash flexibility. Prior research also understated net debt per share (a false **-$1.19 per share** net cash/debt identity); corrected arithmetic shows about **-$12 per share** net of cash.
